concept.conceptName: Potassium [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration

en.name: Urinary potassium
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Urinary potassium is the concentration of potassium in a urine specimen — typically a spot (random) urine sample [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or mEq/L; the two are numerically equivalent for potassium [2]. ## Included Concepts The set lists two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts measuring the potassium concentration in a spot urine specimen: - *3016038 / 2828-2 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: spot (random) urine sample. Most widely used LOINC code. - *3034280 / 35677-4 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: used when the source does not specify whether the sample is spot or timed. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre). Source systems may report `mmol/L` or `mEq/L`; for potassium (a monovalent cation) the two are numerically equal. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for urinary potassium are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Timed urine collections — different specimen (timed vs. spot) - *3010796 / 21476-7 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in 24 hour Urine* - *40760469 / 57355-0 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in 12 hour Urine* - *40760493 / 57379-0 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in 2 hour Urine* ### Excretion rates — different measurand (amount per unit time, not a concentration) - *3016330 / 2829-0 — Potassium [Moles/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *3040170 / 51720-1 — Potassium [Mass/time] in 24 hour Urine* - *40760494 / 57380-8 — Potassium [Moles/time] in 12 hour Urine* - *40762089 / 58996-0 — Potassium [Moles/time] in 18 hour Urine* - *3010303 / 18372-3 — Potassium [Moles/time] in 6 hour Urine* - *3039613 / 53123-6 — Potassium [Moles/time] in 1 hour Urine* - *3017290 / 32550-6 — Potassium [Molar amount] in Urine collected for unspecified duration* ### Ratios — different measurand (ratio to another analyte) - *3014287 / 11148-4 — Potassium/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in Urine* - *3013335 / 13798-4 — Potassium/Creatinine [Mass Ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *3042806 / 33949-9 — Potassium/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in Urine* - *40759264 / 56143-1 — Potassium/Creatinine [Molar ratio] in 24 hour Urine* - *3043518 / 43222-9 — Potassium/Creatinine [Ratio] in Urine* - *40760090 / 56971-5 — Potassium/Creatinine [Ratio] in 24 hour Urine* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "urinary potassium", "urine K", "spot urine potassium" without further specification: `3016038 / 2828-2 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in Urine`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source unit is mEq/L**: numerically equivalent to mmol/L for potassium; no conversion needed. - **Source reports a 24-hour (or other timed) urine collection**: do **not** map to this concept set. Timed urinary potassium is out of scope. - **Source reports a potassium-to-creatinine ratio**: do **not** map. These are different measurands. - **Do not confuse with**: potassium in blood (separate concept set, "Potassium in blood"), urinary potassium excretion rate (mmol/24h). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2828-2 — Potassium [Moles/volume] in Urine. Available: https://loinc.org/2828-2/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
